market_context:
Cohort size: 357 listings scraped in the same market.
Median nightly rate: £175.00
Median 90-day occupancy: 28.3%

title_variants: B&B Close to Leeds City Centre (£117 per night)
Leeds City Centre B&B with Cozy Ambiance
Cosy Leeds City Centre Getaway from £117 pn
analysis.rewritten_desc: Escape to a peaceful retreat in the heart of Leeds, perfect for couples and small families. This charming Bed and Breakfast offers 2 spacious bedrooms, a fully-equipped kitchen, and a comfortable lounge area. Located just a short walk from the vibrant city centre, you'll be surrounded by stunning architecture, lively bars, and world-class restaurants. Take a stroll along the River Aire, visit the iconic Royal Armouries, or explore the historic town hall - all within easy reach. This property boasts a unique blend of traditional charm and modern amenities, making it the perfect base for your next adventure in Leeds. The property has a 4.76 rating from 119 reviews and comes with a £117 per night rate. Come and experience the warm welcome and cozy ambiance that awaits you.
top_amenity_gaps: Outdoor space for al fresco dining or relaxation
Secure Parking for guests with cars
Entertainment options like a TV or game console
analysis.photo_order_rec: The cover photo should be of the living room or kitchen to showcase the cozy ambiance and modern amenities. Alternatively, a photo of the bedroom could work well to highlight the comfort and tranquility of the space.
key_diagnosis: Lower nightly rate (£117) is -33% vs the cohort median, potentially affecting occupancy. Consider increasing the rate to at least £165 per night to be more in line with the market.
Only 18.5% of the next 90 days are booked, significantly below the cohort median occupancy of 28.3%. Analyze the competition and adjust your marketing strategy to attract more bookings.
The listing lacks images and information for potential guests. Add at least 5 high-quality photos, including room types and the property's exterior, to increase guest trust and bookings.
analysis.outreach_hook: With a nightly rate of £117 and a occupancy rate of 18.5% for the next 90 days, there's a clear room for improvement in terms of attracting more bookings.
